sections.paragraph:
ὑπερασπιστής, οῦ, ὁ (ὑπερασπίζω; LXX; PsSol 7:7; JosAs 12:11 [κύριος ὑπʼ τῶν διωκομένων]; Philo, Ebr. 111; Lydus, Mag. 1, 46 p. 48, 22 W.; Hesych., Suda; Jos., Bell. 1, 627) protector 1 Cl 45:7.—DELG s.v. ἀσπίζω.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Lydus Lydus = Joannes Laurentius Lydus, VI a.d.—List 5

Mag.

Mag. = De Magistratibus Populi Romani

mag. = magic(al)

Hesych Hesych , V a.d.—List 5

Suda Suda = a lexical work (freq. cited as Suidas in handbooks); X a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1032
